title: Fix Add Team Member modal — remove permission error and enable saving

requirements.text: Remove the permission check that blocks non-admin users from adding team members in the backend. The `addTeamMember` function should allow any authenticated caller to add a team member without role-based restriction.

quotes: Remove the permission error — fix the logic so logged-in users can add team members without hitting this restriction

acceptanceCriteria: Calling `addTeamMember` no longer returns a permission error for any authenticated user
Team members are successfully saved to the backend state

requirements.text: Update the `AddUserModal` component to remove any frontend-side permission guard or error display (the 'You do not have permission to add team members' message). The modal should show only the clean form with Name, Email ID, and Role fields, plus Cancel and Add Member buttons.

quotes: Keep the form simple — Name, Email ID, and Role fields

acceptanceCriteria: No permission error banner appears in the Add Team Member modal
Modal displays Name, Email ID, and Role fields only
Cancel and Add Member buttons are present and functional
Submitting the form calls the backend and saves the new team member

constraints: Do not change the form fields — Name, Email ID, and Role must remain as-is
Do not modify any files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Redesigning the modal UI beyond removing the permission error
Adding new form fields or validation rules
Changing role options in the dropdown
